claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements JSON repair logic that handles specific Claude key drift patterns and records repairs via json_repaired flag, but stress tests show real Claude agents have not triggered this repair logic yet.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate() attempts JSON.parse with three regex-based repair patterns: line number format (42-88 to "42-88"), backslash escaping, empty key names, and trailing commas
evidence.why: Core repair logic for handling predictable Claude JSON drift

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject() applies three extraction strategies: direct parse, fenced code block (```json), and sliced object extraction, all tracking repaired flag
evidence.why: Multi-strategy extraction ensures JSON is found even when Claude wraps output in markdown or adds prose

evidence.summary: json_repaired field in provider_runs records whether parsedOutput.repaired === true, tracking if any repair occurred
evidence.why: Recording repair events enables auditing whether drift is actually occurring in practice

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult() returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null or providerRun.success is false, with raw_output_excerpt in result
evidence.why: Invalid JSON fails closed with explicit error code and raw output for debugging

evidence.summary: Test 'repairs minor Claude JSON key drift and records the repair' mocks drift patterns: line range format (42-88), escaped backslashes (\.yolo), empty key names (" "summary), and asserts json_repaired === true
evidence.why: Test validates the specific drift patterns the repair logic is designed to handle

evidence.summary: Test 'invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code' validates that 'not json' output returns status=blocked with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code in both agent_results and readiness.blockers
evidence.why: Test confirms fail-closed behavior for unrecoverable JSON

evidence.summary: Stress test provider_runs show json_repaired: false for all three agent roles (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) with parse_error: empty strings, indicating no drift occurred
evidence.why: Real Claude agent execution evidence that repair logic has not been triggered in practice

evidence.summary: Stress test demand problem statement: 'Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch must handle minor Claude JSON key drift and fail closed with explicit blockers on invalid agent JSON'
evidence.why: Evidence this exact demand was previously run and passed PRD readiness after evidence collection

assumptions: The regex repair patterns in parseJsonCandidate cover the actual drift Claude produces, but stress tests have not observed real drift yet
Line 335 regex /("line"\s*:\s*)(\d+)\s*-\s*(\d+)(\s*[,}])/g repairs Claude outputting unquoted line ranges like 42-88 instead of "42-88"
Line 336 regex /\\(?!(["\\/bfnrtu]))/g repairs unescaped backslashes before non-JSON characters
Line 337 regex /([,{]\s*)"\s+"([A-Za-z_][\w-]*)"\s*:/g repairs empty key names with spaces like " "summary
Line 338 regex /,\s*([}\]])/g repairs trailing commas before closing brackets
risks: If real Claude agents produce drift outside these four regex patterns, JSON will be rejected as EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON even though it might be recoverable
The repair logic has not been validated against actual Claude output from stress tests (all show json_repaired: false)

result.notes: Evidence confirms: (1) JSON repair logic exists with four specific regex patterns for line ranges, backslashes, empty keys, and trailing commas. (2) Fail-closed behavior implemented: invalid JSON returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON with explicit blocker. (3) Test coverage validates both repair recording and failure modes. (4) Stress tests show real Claude agents have not triggered repair yet (json_repaired: false across 9 agent runs). Remaining risk: actual drift patterns may differ from the four regex patterns implemented.
